parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'google/gemini-2.0-flash-exp:free', temperature: 0.1, messages: [ { role: 'user', content: [ { type: 'text', text: 'You are a receipt / invoice OCR and data extractor. Read this image (a photo of a receipt or a screenshot of a transaction) and return ONLY a JSON object. No prose, no markdown fences.

' + ($json.caption ? 'User caption: "' + $json.caption + '"

' : '') + 'Schema:
{
  "transaction_type": "income" | "expense",
  "amount": number (grand total, no thousand separators),
  "currency": string (default "IDR"),
  "transaction_date": string (ISO YYYY-MM-DD if visible, else null),
  "merchant_name": string | null,
  "item_name": string | null,
  "category": one of ["Groceries","Dining","Transportation","Utilities","Entertainment","Shopping","Health","Other","Salary","Freelance","Investment"],
  "payment_method": string | null,
  "invoice_number": string | null,
  "notes": string | null,
  "confidence_score": number 0..1
}

Rules:
- Most receipts are expenses. Only use "income" if clearly a refund/credit note.
- amount = grand total after tax/discount
- Use caption as hint for category/notes if useful
- If you cannot read a field, set it to null
- Return ONLY the JSON object.' }, { type: 'image_url', image_url: { url: $json.data_url } } ] } ] }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'openai/gpt-oss-20b:free', messages: [ { role: 'system', content: 'You are a finance data extractor. Extract transaction data from user messages in Indonesian or English. Respond with ONLY a JSON object, no prose, no markdown fences.

Schema:
{
  "transaction_type": "income" | "expense",
  "amount": number,
  "currency": string (default "IDR"),
  "merchant_name": string | null,
  "item_name": string | null,
  "category": one of ["Groceries","Dining","Transportation","Utilities","Entertainment","Shopping","Health","Other","Salary","Freelance","Investment"],
  "payment_method": string | null,
  "notes": string | null,
  "confidence_score": number 0..1
}

Rules:
- "habis", "keluar", "beli", "bayar" -> expense
- "terima", "dapet", "gajian", "masuk" -> income
- "rb" or "k" = x1000, "jt" = x1_000_000
- Amount must be positive number without thousand separators
- If ambiguous, lower confidence_score
- Never return markdown fences.' }, { role: 'user', content: $json.message.text || '' } ], temperature: 0.1 }) }}

parameters.query: select (select user_id from public.user_integrations where channel = 'telegram' and identifier = $1 limit 1) as user_id;

parameters.text:
=⚠️ Sorry, I couldn't read that.

Text example:
• `habis 50rb di warteg buat makan siang`
• `gajian 8jt`
• `bayar listrik 350k`

Or send a clear photo of a receipt.

=🔗 Your Telegram account isn't linked yet.

To link it, open Supabase SQL Editor and run:

```
insert into user_integrations (user_id, channel, identifier, label)
values ('<YOUR_AUTH_USER_ID>', 'telegram', '{{ $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id }}', 'My Telegram');
```

Replace `<YOUR_AUTH_USER_ID>` with your UUID from `auth.users`.
